50 - How To CRUSH The I Need To Ask My Spouse Objection - YouTube.mp4
I'm going to give you an example of how to prevent the spouse objection. So, if you sell to consumers, this is going to be objection you're going to get a lot and less. You learn how to pre-frame this and actually take them out of the frame. We call that a deep frame and then reframe them into a new way of thinking, which is results-based thinking. I'm also gonna show you a pre-frame, okay? So let's say that you have a booked appointment. You a random example you have a booked appointment it could be a meeting in person could be on zoom could be a call okay and let's say in this example I'm just going to give you this is well I'm going to show you example first okay and then I'll show you maybe a few industry specific here so you can kind of see the I want you to see the format so you just plug in what you sell okay now Now, let's say I get on there.
I'm not gonna ask this question right off the bat, but maybe three, four, five minutes in, I might say, so when I find out they have a spouse or whatever, I'm gonna say, Does your spouse know you're in this meeting or are you on like a secret mission? Okay. See how I'm playful there. Oh, no, no, no. She knows I'm on here. No, he knows I'm here. He's like, no, no, no. I'm on a secret mission. Okay. You're on a secret mission. How did your spouse feel about you? And you repeat back the problem they already said. So typically, if you haven't helped them find problems yet, you won't use this yet. Okay, so I wouldn't use this until you're probably in problem awareness. So I'm going to scratch that.
Don't use this three minutes in. Once you're in problem awareness, you've helped him find problems, okay? You've helped him feel the pain, relive the pain of their current situation and have that fear of future pain. Then you're going to use this frame. Okay. So then I'm going to go in. So how did your spouse feel about you? And you repeat back the problem. How does your spouse feel about you? Like only getting. Two listings a month, let's say. If I'm a marketing agency and I'm talking to a real estate agent about better leads for more listings, they say they're only getting two listings a month, but they want six. Okay. How does your spouse feel about you only getting one listing a month or whatever it is? Oh no, he doesn't like that.
Right. But, but I mean, does, does he want you to get six listings a month or just stay at one? No, no. He wants me to get six listings. See? So I focus on how do they feel about the problem or consequence, and then do they want you to and you repeat back the result they said they want it okay? So in this example, let's say if you sell network marketing or you sell affiliate marketing or like some type of business opportunity or franchise, okay? How does your spouse feel? Let's say the problem was they're driving hour and a half each way to get to work and they're working 12 hours a day, hours a day at the job, and it's just burning them out. I mean, they're spending 14, 15 hours a day.
They just have no family time. So I might come back. I mean, how did your spouse feel about you having to work 12 hours a day and then drive back and forth another three hours a day. Oh, she doesn't like it, blah, blah, blah. I mean, does she want you to be able to have more time? At home with the kids so you can watch them grow up? Of course she does. Now, that's a pre-fame. It's hard for him or her to come back at the end and say, 'I need to talk to my spouse about starting my own business where I can have more time with my family.' It's harder for them to say it. And if they still do say it, it's very easy to take them out of that frame and reframe them into a new frame of...
Why would you have to talk to your spouse about you being at home with them or whatever, if you sell an album or that'd be a different example. Okay. Or I could, or let's say if you're a recruit. And the job offer you have for them is where they can work from home. You work for a recruiting agency. So how does your spouse feel about you having to drive an hour and a half each way, where you're not hardly around? Oh, she doesn't like it. How does your spouse feel about you being gone four days a week at the job, flying back and forth to New York? Oh, she doesn't like it. And I can probe off that and clarify, help him or her relive the pain. Then I'm going to come around.
Does she want you to work from home where you can have time to actually help her with the kids? Oh, for sure she wants that. See, I'm... Pre-framing this okay so once I find the problems, doesn't matter the industry, it is okay. Let's say, if I sold SAS to associations that helped them go from manually keeping score of like, you know, contests or events or whatever to streamline it and make it automated, where it saves you know, their department— let's say, two to three hours a day— where they get other projects done. Same thing. How does your CEO feel about you guys having to spend three hours a day manually doing this, where it takes you away from other projects? Oh, she doesn't like it.
But I mean, does she want you to like get this streamlined? So you can actually focus on other projects? Oh, definitely. See, same thing. Don't matter, okay? So that's an example of the pre-frame here. And there's an example of I'm... deframing them. How did your spouse feel about the problems you know and you know, or the the challenges or the consequences? Here's another deframe. Do they want you to repeat back the end result? And now I start to reframe. Okay, this would be like a reframe here. This is part of the pre-frame. This is part of the pre-frame. This is part of the free frame. The whole process is actually— this is part of the whole reframing process. That's just called a pre-frame. Those types of questions to help prevent objection.
But all I'm doing is reframing them away from at the end I need to talk to my spouse to a new belief system of why would I need to talk to my spouse he or she already wants all these things I said I wanted. So reframing is just the process of taking them out of their belief systems or frames. And then putting them in new belief systems. And for more info on that, make sure you go through one of the first training videos I did in the course where I talk about the power of... of every prospect or every human lives in frames, right? Or belief systems, and we have to take them out of those. We have to deframe them and reframe them into new belief systems or new ways of thinking, all right. All right, hope that helped you today, and I'll see you on the next training video.
